title: Capture Observations for Later Diagnosis While Working to Restore Operation
summary: Document operator-reported actions, reported Aviva status, and observed backend system state during an incident so later diagnosis is possible while immediate support remains focused on restoring operation.
when_to_use: Use during a live support incident when immediate restoration is the priority and support needs to capture enough observations to enable later diagnosis, especially when Aviva-reported actions or statuses need to be compared against RMS, WCS, or OptiSweep state.
not_for: Do not use this procedure as a complete root-cause diagnosis method during the live incident when the source states support should not expect to 100% diagnose what happened in the moment.
Do not use this procedure to claim direct Aviva verification when support lacks Aviva access; in that case the source supports relying on operator-reported information only.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Capture observations, compare reported and observed states, and preserve enough detail for later diagnosis while prioritizing restoration.
Report what was pressed or sent in Aviva and describe the status visible in Aviva when support does not have direct access.

safety_notes: The source emphasizes restoration of operation during high-priority incidents; observation capture should support, not delay, getting the system back running.
Do not overstate conclusions during the live incident; the source says support should not expect to fully diagnose the issue in the moment.
access_or_tools_needed: Observed system state from RMS, WCS, or OptiSweep
Operator-reported Aviva information when direct access is unavailable

access_required: Access to incident notes or ticketing method
Access to RMS, WCS, or OptiSweep state visibility as available
Operator contact able to report Aviva actions and status when support lacks Aviva access
system_preconditions: An active incident or support call is in progress
There is a need to compare operator-reported Aviva actions against backend system state

visual_references.description: Training video frame covering Aviva access limitations, RMS as first check, WCS/OptiSweep state comparison, and note-taking for later diagnosis.

steps.title: Record operator-reported Aviva action
steps.purpose: Capture what action the operator says was taken in Aviva when support may not have direct Aviva access.
steps.instruction: Ask the operator what they pressed or sent from Aviva, if applicable, and record that action in the incident notes. If support does not have direct Aviva access, explicitly note that the action record is based on operator report.
steps.expected_result: The incident record contains the operator-reported Aviva action or command attempt.
steps.healthy_condition: A clear note exists describing the operator-reported Aviva action and whether it is directly observed or operator-reported.
steps.failure_condition: The action taken in Aviva is not recorded, or the note does not distinguish operator report from direct observation.

actions.label: Capture reported Aviva action
actions.exact_action: Record what the operator says they pressed or sent from Aviva, if applicable.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Guidance that non-UPS support users may not have Aviva access and should rely on the operator to report what was done in Aviva.

stop_or_escalate_if: Support cannot determine what action was attempted in Aviva.
There is no direct Aviva access and the operator cannot provide a reliable description of what was pressed or sent.

source_evidence.text: Record what the operator says they pressed or sent from Aviva, if applicable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Anybody who's not a UPS user doesn't have access to Aviva. The best thing you will have, you'll have an operator who you can tell where to look in Aviva.
This segment covers support guidance for Aviva-related incidents and clarifies that most support users do not have direct access to Aviva.

steps.title: Record operator-reported Aviva status
steps.purpose: Preserve the status visible to the operator in Aviva so it can later be compared against backend system state.
steps.instruction: Ask the operator what status they see in Aviva and record that reported status in the incident notes. If support cannot directly view Aviva, note that the status is operator-reported.
steps.expected_result: The incident record contains the operator-reported Aviva status.
steps.healthy_condition: The reported Aviva status is documented clearly enough to compare with RMS, WCS, or OptiSweep state.
steps.failure_condition: The Aviva status is missing, vague, or not identified as operator-reported when direct access is unavailable.

actions.label: Capture reported Aviva status
actions.exact_action: Record the status the operator reports seeing in Aviva.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Support guidance that the operator may need to describe what is visible in Aviva because support does not have direct access.

stop_or_escalate_if: The operator cannot identify the status shown in Aviva.
The reported Aviva status is too incomplete to compare against backend state.

source_evidence.text: Record the status the operator reports seeing in Aviva.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The best thing you will have, you'll have an operator who you can tell where to look in Aviva.
If Aviva put it in startup, but we don't see any startup, it's a communication issue.

steps.title: Record observed backend system state
steps.purpose: Capture the actual state visible in RMS, WCS, or OptiSweep so support can compare backend behavior against the reported Aviva action or status.
steps.instruction: Check the available backend system views and record the state observed in RMS, WCS, OptiSweep, or the RMS screen, including whether the expected state change is present. Where applicable, begin with the RMS screen to see whether any system faults are present.
steps.expected_result: The incident record contains observed backend state and any visible system fault information.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed backend state is documented clearly, including whether expected state changes are visible and whether system faults are present.
steps.failure_condition: Backend state is not checked, not recorded, or does not include whether expected state changes or system faults are visible.

actions.label: Inspect RMS for system faults
actions.exact_action: First thing first, go to that RMS screen, see if any system faults.

actions.label: Inspect WCS or OptiSweep state
actions.exact_action: See in WCS or in our OptiSweep what is the state of the system.

actions.label: Record observed backend state
actions.exact_action: Record the state observed in WCS, OptiSweep, or the RMS screen, including whether expected state changes are present.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training guidance on checking RMS first and comparing WCS or OptiSweep state against Aviva-triggered actions.
Overall System RMS page showing system faults on the left and active AGV faults on the right.
Overall System RMS page reference and note that visibility may be blank when firewall access is blocked.

stop_or_escalate_if: RMS, WCS, or OptiSweep state cannot be accessed or viewed.
The RMS page is unavailable or blank and backend state cannot be confirmed from available tools.
System faults are present and require continued incident handling beyond note capture.

source_evidence.text: Record the state observed in WCS, OptiSweep, or the RMS screen, including whether expected state changes are present.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: First thing first, go to that RMS screen, see if any system faults. This should always be your first step on a phone call.
The best thing would be to see in WCS or in our OptiSweep what is the state of the system.
Overall System RMS page as an initial place to check for system and active AGV faults.
The page should show RMS but may appear blank because the firewall is not open.

steps.title: Note mismatches between reported and observed state
steps.purpose: Identify and preserve discrepancies between what the operator reports from Aviva and what backend systems show.
steps.instruction: Compare the operator-reported Aviva action or status against the observed state in WCS, OptiSweep, or RMS, and note any mismatch between them.
steps.expected_result: The incident record clearly identifies whether the reported Aviva state matches or does not match backend system state.
steps.healthy_condition: Any mismatch is documented specifically enough to support later diagnosis, such as a reported startup not appearing in backend state.
steps.failure_condition: Potential mismatches are not recorded, or the notes do not distinguish between reported and observed state.

actions.label: Compare reported and observed state
actions.exact_action: Note any mismatch between operator-reported status and system-reported status.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Example guidance that if Aviva put the system in startup but WCS or OptiSweep does not show startup, the issue may be a communication mismatch.

stop_or_escalate_if: A mismatch is observed but cannot be described clearly enough for later review.
The reported Aviva state and backend state cannot be compared because one side of the comparison is unavailable.

source_evidence.text: Note any mismatch between operator-reported status and system-reported status.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If Aviva put it in startup, but we don't see any startup, it's a communication issue.

steps.title: Document observations for later diagnosis without overstating diagnosis
steps.purpose: Ensure the incident record is detailed enough for later review while keeping live support focused on restoration rather than complete diagnosis.
steps.instruction: Document the captured actions, statuses, observed system state, and mismatches in enough detail that the issue can be reviewed later. Do not claim a complete root-cause diagnosis during the live incident if the source evidence does not support it.
steps.expected_result: A usable incident record exists for later diagnosis while live support remains focused on restoring operation.
steps.healthy_condition: Notes are detailed enough for later review and clearly separate observations from conclusions.
steps.failure_condition: Notes are too sparse for later diagnosis, or they overstate certainty about root cause during the live incident.

actions.label: Finalize observation notes
actions.exact_action: Document the observations in enough detail that the issue can be reviewed later, without claiming a complete root-cause diagnosis during the live incident.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training guidance that support should take enough notes to diagnose later while focusing on getting the system back running.

stop_or_escalate_if: Observation notes are incomplete and cannot support later diagnosis.
Support is being pushed to provide a complete diagnosis that is not supported by the observations gathered.

source_evidence.text: Document the observations in enough detail that the issue can be reviewed later, without claiming a complete root-cause diagnosis during the live incident.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The goal of support should always be not to 100% diagnose what happened, but to take enough notes so that we can diagnose it later.
If there is a problem, the stage is for us to diagnose why it happened, and for the support to take notes on this is what was observed.
Support is described as focusing on capturing observations for later diagnosis while prioritizing restoration of operation during P1 incidents under SLA pressure.

success_criteria: A record exists of what the operator says was pressed or sent from Aviva, if applicable.
A record exists of the status the operator reported seeing in Aviva.
Observed state from RMS, WCS, or OptiSweep is documented, including whether expected state changes are present.
Any mismatch between operator-reported status and backend system-reported status is documented.
The notes are detailed enough to support later diagnosis without requiring a complete live root-cause determination.
healthy_conditions: Support has a usable record of observed actions, statuses, and mismatches.
Observation notes clearly distinguish operator-reported information from directly observed backend state.
Live support remains focused on restoring operation while preserving evidence for later review.
failure_conditions: Support attempts to fully diagnose the issue in the moment instead of capturing observations for later diagnosis.
Observation notes are incomplete or omit key reported actions, statuses, or mismatches.
Aviva information is recorded without noting that it came from operator reports when direct access is unavailable.
Backend state is not checked or not documented.
escalation_guidance: If observations are incomplete because support lacks direct Aviva access, explicitly note that the information came from operator reports.
If RMS, WCS, or OptiSweep state cannot be accessed, record that limitation in the incident notes.
If the issue cannot be fully diagnosed during the live incident, preserve observations and continue prioritizing restoration as supported by the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary training segment for Aviva access limits, RMS first check, state comparison, and note-taking expectations.
Overall System RMS page showing where system and active AGV faults appear.
Overall System RMS page reference including note that the page may be blank if firewall access is blocked.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The goal of support should always be not to 100% diagnose what happened, but to take enough notes so that we can diagnose it later.
If there is a problem, the stage is for us to diagnose why it happened, and for the support to take notes on this is what was observed.

missing_details: The source does not provide a specific incident note template, ticket fields, or logging system.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this documentation.
The source does not define formal escalation routing, only guidance to preserve observations and continue restoration focus.
The source does not provide direct Aviva screen artifacts in this packet; Aviva information is supported through operator-report guidance.
